data.title: Rivian CEO's $402M pay package hinges on aggressive growth targets
data.description:
Rivian Automotive (RIVN) CEO RJ Scaringe received a compensation package of about $402.6M for 2025, driven largely by a newly approved equity award designed to tie his long-term pay to the company’s performance, according to a regulatory filing on Monday. 

The package includes roughly $373.5M in option awards, $26.6M in stock awards, and about $1.12M in base salary. The equity grants make most of the compensation contingent on future milestones.

The award is part of a broader pay plan unveiled in November that could be worth as much as $4.6B over a decade if performance conditions are met. In November, Tesla (TSLA) shareholders also approved a performance award of $1T for CEO Elon Musk. 

Rivian’s compensation committee said the structure aims to retain CEO Scaringe and align incentives with shareholders as the company enters its next phase, including scaling production and launching its R2 platform.

The options vest only if Rivian meets a series of ambitious targets tied to stock price, profitability, and cash generation. 

Equity tranches linked to share-price hurdles require the stock to rise from $40 to as high as $140 over sustained periods, which is a premium of up to 740% from stock's Monday close of $16.7. Additional portions depend on achieving adjusted operating income and cash flow from operations targets through 2032.

The company also scrapped a 2021 CEO performance award. The committee said the new structure better reflects current market conditions and Scaringe’s existing ownership stake while providing stronger incentives to execute on Rivian’s long-term strategy.

Scaringe, who founded Rivian in 2009 and led it to a 2021 public listing, remains central to the company’s push to transition from a startup to a scaled electric-vehicle manufacturer.Rivian shares have lost -15% value so far this year.

data.title: Smithfield Foods beats Q1 estimates
data.description:
• Smithfield Foods, Inc. press release (SFD): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.64 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $3.8B (+0.5% Y/Y) beats by $100M.

  • The Company is reaffirming its financial outlook provided on March 24, 2026 as follows:

    • Total Company sales to be up low-single-digits compared to fiscal year 2025.
    • Packaged Meats segment adjusted operating profit of between $1,100 million to $1,200 million.
    • Fresh Pork segment adjusted operating profit of between $200 million to $260 million.
    • Hog Production segment adjusted operating profit of between $150 million to $200 million.
    • Total Company adjusted operating profit of between $1,325 million to $1,475 million.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Novartis Q1 EPS and revenue trails estimates; maintains 2026 growth outlook
Stock index futures mixed ahead of more earnings
data.description:
Stock index futures were mixed before the bell as investors awaited more earnings while U.S.-Iran updates remained in the forefront.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.41%, S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.12%, while Dow Jones Industrial Average futures (INDU) +0.16%.

On the earnings front, General Motors (GM) and Coca-Cola (KO) are set to report results, while investors also look ahead to the Conference Board’s consumer confidence data for further clues on the economic outlook.

Geopolitical tensions remained in focus after Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrived in Russia, where he is expected to meet President Vladimir Putin, even as negotiations with the U.S. reached a stalemate over the weekend.

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose to 3.83%, while the 10-year yield (US10Y) climbed to 4.37% and the 30-year yield (US30Y) advanced to 4.96%, reflecting upward pressure as investors assessed the macro backdrop.

Top gainers included IDEXX Laboratories (IDXX) +17.89%, Nucor (NUE) +3.64%, and Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY) +3.30%. Decliners included PulteGroup (PHM) -7.32%, Pentair (PNR) -4.35%, and Oracle (ORCL) -3.10%.

data.title: AstraZeneca’s Breztri wins U.S. approval for asthma treatment
data.description:
• AstraZeneca’s (AZN) fixed-dose triple-combination therapy Breztri Aerosphere has been approved in the U.S. for the maintenance treatment of asthma in adult and pediatric patients 12 years of age and older, the company said on Tuesday.
  • Breztri is a single-inhaler that combines the efficacy of corticosteroid/long-acting beta2-agonist (ICS/LABA) medicines with a long-acting muscarinic antagonist. 
  • Breztri (320/18/9.6μg) was approved in the U.S. in 2020 to treat adults with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease and was prescribed to more than 6.8 million patients globally in 2025.
  • “Despite the availability of dual maintenance therapy, many patients are still at risk for exacerbations and experience daily breathing difficulties, reduced lung function and the ongoing fear of worsening symptoms," Njira Lugogo from the Department of Internal Medicine, University of Michigan, said.
  • "The FDA approval of Breztri as the only maintenance triple therapy for people with asthma 12 years of age and older marks a pivotal moment in helping those living with this debilitating disease breathe better, sooner.”

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: BP exceeds Q1 estimates, announces Q2 and reaffirms FY26 outlook and quarterly dividend
DoubleU offers $184M to acquire remaining DoubleDown Interactive stake
data.description:
• DoubleU Games to acquire all remaining publicly held ADSs of DoubleDown Interactive (DDI) in an all‑cash transaction.
  • DoubleU Games is offering $11.25 in cash per ADS, valuing the transaction at around $184M. Each ADS represents 1/20 of one DDI common share, so the offer effectively covers the ~32.95% of DDI’s common shares not already owned by DoubleU.
  • If completed, DDI would become a wholly owned subsidiary of DoubleU Games, de‑listed from the Nasdaq.
  • DoubleU Games currently holds ~67.1% of DDI's outstanding common shares.
  • The stock price jumps about 12% on Tuesday during pre-market hours.

data.title: China’s Q1 urban job creation hits 2.99M as unemployment steadies at 5.3%
data.description:
China added 2.99 million new urban jobs in the first quarter of 2026, keeping the surveyed urban unemployment rate at an average of 5.3%, according to the Ministry of Human Resources and Social Security.

The performance remains well within Beijing's full-year target of approximately 5.5% unemployment and over 12 million new jobs.

Ministry spokesperson Cui Pengcheng noted that efforts are currently focused on the "critical window" for college graduates entering the market. To support this, the government facilitated 59,000 job fairs offering 36 million positions and provided 1 million transportation trips to ensure migrant worker mobility during the spring recruitment season.

On Tuesday, the Shanghai Composite edged down 0.1% to 4,082, while the Shenzhen Component fell 0.8% to 14,869.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSE:TDF), (NYSEARCA:CHIQ), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:KBA), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:CWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:KURE).

data.title: European markets flat on stalled U.S.-Iran talks
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.17% to 10,339. 

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.05% to 24,073. 

France (CAC:IND) -0.01% to 8,140. 

In other parts of Europe, retail trade in Spain rose by 4.1% Y/Y in March. Spain’s unemployment rate climbed to 10.83% in Q1.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) traded 0.19% lower to 607.8, marking a seventh straight session of losses. The investor sentiment remained cautious as tensions in the Middle East persisted, the Strait of Hormuz stayed largely closed, and oil prices continued to climb. The euro held near $1.17, lingering close to the two-week lows seen last week, as investors assessed the fallout from a diplomatic standoff in U.S.-Iran negotiations.

On the corporate front, Novartis (NVS) shares fell after reporting sales below expectations. BP (BP) rose as its profit more than doubled, while Barclays (BCS) dropped.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was up 2 basis points to 4.36%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 5.00%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 3.05%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: U.S. is being humiliated by Iran, lacks strategy in negotiations - Germany's Merz
data.description:
German Chancellor ‌Friedrich Merz has said the Iranian leadership is humiliating the U.S., which "quite obviously went into this war without any strategy," amid a lack of progress in negotiations to end the conflict.

"The Iranians are obviously negotiating very skilfully — or simply very skilfully not negotiating," Merz said during a visit to a school in western Germany. "A whole nation is being humiliated by the Iranian leadership."

He added that the Americans "have no truly convincing strategy in the negotiations," pointing to U.S. officials traveling to Pakistan earlier this month and leaving without results.

Merz reiterated that Europe was not consulted before the U.S. and Israel launched a joint attack on Iran on Feb. 28, saying he expressed his skepticism directly to Trump afterward.

"If I'd known that this would go on for five or six weeks and get progressively worse, I would've made my point to him more forcefully," the chancellor ‌said. "The problem with conflicts like this is always the same – you don't just get in, you also have to get out. We saw that very painfully in Afghanistan and Iraq."

Merz added that the war has directly impacted Germany's economy and is "costing us a great deal of money," calling for an end to the war "as soon as possible."

His unusually sharp rebuke comes as U.S.-Iran negotiations make little headway. Iran offered to reopen the Strait of Hormuz if the U.S. lifted its blockade of the country's ports and postponed nuclear talks. But the U.S. is reportedly unwilling to accept the offer.

-- The Associated Press contributed to this report.
